content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(i) WARN — Privacy policy explicitly states "We do not collect, process, or store your location data" yet RunSessionManager.swift imports CoreLocation, requests whenAuthorization, and actively records GPS location during runs (App/RunSessionManager.swift:1-13), and the app description advertises "maps every run with GPS" (fastlane/metadata/en-US/description.txt:1).
Pierre: This is a direct contradiction that Apple reviewers actively look for — the app collects and stores precise location data (route history) while the published privacy policy denies any location collection at all. This mismatch could cause outright rejection under 5.1.1(i) since the policy must "clearly and explicitly" identify what data is collected. Fix by updating the privacy policy to disclose location/GPS collection, storage (device/iCloud), retention, and deletion, and ensure the App Privacy "Nutrition Label" in App Store Connect also lists Location as collected data.
